mentions.note: Medical device sector unpopular in current market; no attractive opportunities even among quality peers like Medtronic, Intuitive Surgical, Boston Scientific.
Up 4.5% as part of software rotation but Cramer doubts move sustainability; short squeeze pattern evident across group.
Acquitted themselves well with up 3 bucks but trades at high multiple; Motorola competition still concerning; market doesn't favor high multiple stocks.
Club retailers have diverged; BJ's lagging significantly; Costco clear winner among warehouse operators.
160-year-old power equipment maker resurgent with data center boom; 44% revenue growth, $2.7B backlog, 2000% booking growth YoY; can deliver in under 36 months vs 5-10 for turbines.
Integral to data center construction but trades at 36x earnings like tech stock; valuation has become overheated despite division excitement.
Gotten red hot; warehouse club significantly outperforming; preferred long-term choice over BJ's Wholesale Club.
Up 3.5% in software rotation but sustainability highly uncertain; short squeeze pattern repeats for enterprise software group.
Retail bust from elevated interest rates; housing market weakness persists; desktop down significantly with no relief expected.

Neutral atom quantum computing company with differentiated technology; major defense partnerships (NASA, Pentagon, L3Harris, Safran, Nvidia); monetizing near-term sensing while building quantum computing; CEO clearly articulates complex story; clear path to profitability.
Do-it-yourself focused; better positioned than Home Depot in weak housing market; preferred choice for retail exposure.
Profitable semiconductor serving data center power and cooling needs, but recently spiked higher (likely short squeeze); valuation at 90x earnings is stretched; better entry points likely elsewhere.
Led software rotation up 3% but move feels like giant short squeeze; all prior enterprise software rallies reversed after strong initial days.
Jumped 5% in software rally but gains likely temporary; history shows enterprise software moves meet torrential selling after initial strength.
Pure speculative play transitioning from gravity storage to AI infrastructure; loses money and lacks sustainable revenue; low share price limits downside but profitability highly questionable.
Most important stock in market tied to data center buildout; own don't trade it; spectacular growth and awesome earnings call expected.
Incredibly good company whose stock got ahead of itself; expensive but amazing fundamentals; hold position and let it run.
Loses significant money with minimal revenue; unsustainable business model with fundamental profitability issues make it uninvestable.
Business case dependent on Federal Reserve rate cuts unlikely given oil-driven inflation preventing disinflation; fundamental headwinds limit upside.

Upcoming IPO valued at $1.75-2T on $8-18.5B revenue (80x sales) vs. profitable tech peers; risks bubble to $5-6T market cap if underwriters limit float, exceeding Nvidia despite vastly lower profitability; monitor for irrational IPO mania.
New management reversing performance trends; approaching favorable comparisons after three-month surge; terrific results expected.
High-end home builder struggling in rising rate environment; very tough to own when rates climbing; down less than peers but fundamentals challenged.
Cooling equipment supplier for data centers; stock already up huge; investor meeting may not provide additional upside catalyst.
SaaS company rallied 5% in software rotation; uncertainty on sustainability given history of torrential selling after enterprise software rallies.
Goldman downgraded to neutral with $52 target from buy; Cramer very concerned; sees no relief for home appliance demand; prefers Home Depot or Lowe's.
Among greatest companies of era; revamped for all income levels; excellent selection and pricing; terrific earnings expected.
